qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public directory shares resolve against the parent directory and expose sibling files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable `withHashFile` branch in `http/public.go`: directory shares call `filepath.Dir(link.Path)` before `afero.NewBasePathFs`, so public share requests resolve from the parent directory and expose sibling files through the same public listing and download endpoints described by the advisory.

selected_finding.title: Public directory shares resolve against the parent directory and expose sibling files

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's exact vulnerable code and root cause in `http/public.go`, and the copied PoC achieved the advisory-described unauthorized sibling-directory listing and sibling-file download through the public share endpoints.
evidence: The advisory and upstream fix both identify the directory-share logic in `http/public.go`, where `basePath = filepath.Dir(basePath)` was corrected to `filepath.Clean(link.Path)` in commit `31194fb57a5b92e7155219d7ec7273028fcb2e83`.

The finding report traces the same vulnerable source-to-sink path: `withHashFile` sets the wrong `BasePathFs` root for directory shares, and `/api/public/share/{hash}` plus `/api/public/dl/{hash}/{path}` then expose sibling entries outside the intended shared directory.

The copied PoC run log under `.judge/poc_runs/public-share-parent-directory-disclosure/run.log` shows exploit success: the unauthenticated public listing leaked `otherdir`, `secret.txt`, and `shared`, and the sibling download returned the out-of-scope proof string `sibling-secret`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied reproduction created a public share for `/parent/shared`, then an unauthenticated request listed parent-scope sibling entries (`otherdir`, `secret.txt`, `shared`) and downloaded `/parent/secret.txt`, returning `sibling-secret`.
